items.title: Set 01 · Pregunta 1
items.prompt: Don't forget_____ English at least ______ a week
items.content_es: Don't forget_____ English at least ______ a week
items.explanation_es: Pregunta de simulacro convertida a formato tipo test con explicaciones por alternativa.

option_explanations.label: studying / one time
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: en imperativo, la estructura es forget to + infinitive (no *forget + -ing*). Además, *one time a week* suena poco natural aquí; lo estándar es *once a week*.

option_explanations.label: to study / once
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: Don't forget to study + frecuencia once a week.

option_explanations.label: to study / one
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: *to study* está bien, pero one a week está incompleto. Sería *once a week* o *one day/time a week*. -

option_explanations.label: a lots of
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: opción añadida a partir del mismo bloque de examen; no completa correctamente la estructura pedida.

items.title: Set 01 · Pregunta 2
items.prompt: There have been ____ opportunities to learn English
items.content_es: There have been ____ opportunities to learn English
items.explanation_es: Pregunta de simulacro convertida a formato tipo test con explicaciones por alternativa.

option_explanations.label: a lots of
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: la forma correcta es a lot of (sin *s*) o lots of (sin *a*).

option_explanations.label: plenty of
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: plenty of + plural contable (*opportunities*).

option_explanations.label: too much
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: too much se usa con incontables; con plurales contables sería too many. Además, cambia el sentido a "demasiadas". -

option_explanations.label: studying / one time
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: opción añadida a partir del mismo bloque de examen; no completa correctamente la estructura pedida.

items.title: Set 01 · Pregunta 3
items.prompt: Louise is married ___ an important doctor
items.content_es: Louise is married ___ an important doctor
items.explanation_es: Pregunta de simulacro convertida a formato tipo test con explicaciones por alternativa.

option_explanations.label: to
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: estructura fija be married to.

option_explanations.label: with
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: *married with* no se usa para indicar con quién estás casado/a.

option_explanations.label: a and b
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: no pueden ser "a y b" porque b es incorrecta. ●

option_explanations.label: studying / one time
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: opción añadida a partir del mismo bloque de examen; no completa correctamente la estructura pedida.

items.title: Set 01 · Pregunta 4
items.prompt: She was living ___ an important British airport
items.content_es: She was living ___ an important British airport
items.explanation_es: Pregunta de simulacro convertida a formato tipo test con explicaciones por alternativa.

option_explanations.label: closer than
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: *closer than* es comparativo y exige comparación (*closer than X*). No funciona como preposición "cerca de" aquí.

option_explanations.label: next of
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: la expresión correcta es next to, no *next of*.

option_explanations.label: near
option_explanations.explanation: Correcta: near + place = "cerca de". ●

option_explanations.label: studying / one time
option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: opción añadida a partir del mismo bloque de examen; no completa correctamente la estructura pedida.

items.title: Set 01 · Pregunta 5
items.prompt: I'd prefer ___at the weekends
items.content_es: I'd prefer ___at the weekends
items.explanation_es: Pregunta de simulacro convertida a formato tipo test con explicaciones por alternativa.

option_explanations.label: working

option_explanations.explanation: Incorrecta: out solo no funciona aquí. ## READING 1 — MURDER MYSTERY Once, when I was teaching English to children in Cambridge, I died while eating my dinner. The children were eleven to fourteen years old and there were about one hundred and twenty of them in the large hall of a boarding school. We were all eating ice cream when ## I made strange noises and fell forward onto my plate. This was not some unusual way of teaching the children about crime, but a murder mystery game. It was a bit like a play in the theatre, but the whole school was involved. All of the teachers were either victims or suspects and all of the children were detectives trying to find out who the murderer was. The children knew the situation was serious as many teachers were “dying”, so they were all very excited. Lying next to my ice cream, I loved being a “victim”. However, I had to remember not to smile as the teachers dragged me across the floor to get rid of my dead body so the students knew ## I was really “dead”. This was just the start of my murder mystery career! Soon after that, one of my friends bought a murder mystery game and invited me and several other friends to a dinner party. We all had to dress up as different characters. The story was that one of our close friends had been murdered and we were all suspects. We had to tell the other characters important information and question people about the information they gave. Then, at the end of the evening, we tried to work out who had done it. I wasn’t very good at the police investigation part, but ## I loved playing a part and the excitement of knowing that one of the people in the room was a “murderer”. Now I run my own company - Murder Mystery Occasions. I organise murder mystery parties in restaurants or historic buildings. Usually ## I hire a private room so that the “murder” doesn’t upset other guests. Castles are particularly popular with my guests because they’re so atmospheric, so I use them if they’re available. ## I write the scripts and employ actors to be some of the key characters. People pay to come along and join in with the fun while having a wonderful dinner. The murder usually happens somewhere between the starter and the main course and there is always a lot of excitement when the police arrive to take fingerprints and interview key suspects. People tell me that’s the best part. Then, at the end of the evening, there is a prize for the table that guesses who is the murderer, but everybody who comes along seems to enjoy the drama. So, you could say I’ve made a good, honest career out of murder. ##

items.title: Set 03 · Pregunta 28
items.prompt: READING — PREGUNTAS Y SU RESPUESTA ## EXPLICADA # 1. What primarily contributed to the students’ heightened excitement during the event in Cambridge? Answer: According to the text, the students became especially excited because they saw several teachers pretending to die, which made the whole situation feel much more serious and realistic. Explicación de la paráfrasis: Aquí no copiamos literalmente “realistic portrayal of multiple teachers ‘dying’ throughout the game” , sino que lo convertimos en una respuesta más natural: “they saw several teachers pretending to die” . Además, “heightened excitement” pasa a “became especially excited” , que es una forma más simple y más ## B1. La idea clave que debes mantener es: los alumnos se emocionan por el realismo de ver a varios profesores “muriendo” . # 2. In the narrator’s early experience at the dinner-party mystery, what aspect of the game appealed to them the most? Answer: Based on the text, the narrator most enjoyed playing a character and feeling the suspense of having a possible murderer within the group. Explicación de la paráfrasis: La pregunta usa una formulación más compleja: “immersive role-playing and the suspense of being among potential murderers” . La respuesta buena la simplifica a: “playing a character” y “feeling the suspense” . También “among potential murderers” se transforma en “within the group” , que suena más natural y evita copiar. La idea base es: lo que más le gustó fue interpretar un papel y sentir la tensión de tener al culpable entre ellos . # 3. What does the narrator imply about the logistics of hosting murder mystery events in public places? Answer: As explained in the text, these events have to be held in private spaces so that other customers are not disturbed. Explicación de la paráfrasis: Aquí la clave es no repetir literalmente “must be kept separate to avoid alarming uninvolved guests” . Se puede llevar a una versión más clara y directa: “have to be held in private spaces so that other customers are not disturbed” . “uninvolved guests” se simplifica a “other customers” y “kept separate” a “held in private spaces” . La idea importante es: se hacen en zonas privadas para no molestar a la gente ajena al evento . # 4. Why does the narrator particularly value historic venues such as castles for their events? Answer: According to the text, the narrator values castles because their atmosphere makes the mystery more dramatic and immersive. Explicación de la paráfrasis: En vez de copiar “their ambience intensifies the dramatic impact” , la respuesta lo convierte en algo más sencillo: “their atmosphere makes the mystery more dramatic and immersive” . “historic venues such as castles” también se reduce a “castles” , porque con eso ya basta para responder. La idea central es: los castillos gustan por el ambiente especial que crean . 5. What feature of the narrator’s events seems to be the audience’s favourite element? Answer: Based on the text, the audience’s favourite part is when the fake police arrive, collect evidence, and question the main suspects. Explicación de la paráfrasis: La opción habla de “the arrival of the ‘police,’ who gather evidence and interrogate suspects” . La respuesta correcta lo expresa de forma más natural y más simple: “when the fake police arrive, collect evidence, and question the main suspects” . Aquí cambias “interrogate” por “question” , que es más ## B1, y “gather evidence” por “collect evidence” , que mantiene el mismo significado. La idea clave es: la parte favorita del público es la llegada de la policía y la investigación . ## IDIOMS # 1. Original: The politician avoided answering the question directly. Rephrase using an idiom related to crime: The politician ______

La idea importante es: se hacen en zonas privadas para no molestar a la gente ajena al evento . # 4. Why does the narrator particularly value historic venues such as castles for their events? Answer: According to the text, the narrator values castles because their atmosphere makes the mystery more dramatic and immersive. Explicación de la paráfrasis: En vez de copiar “their ambience intensifies the dramatic impact” , la respuesta lo convierte en algo más sencillo: “their atmosphere makes the mystery more dramatic and immersive” . “historic venues such as castles” también se reduce a “castles” , porque con eso ya basta para responder. La idea central es: los castillos gustan por el ambiente especial que crean . 5. What feature of the narrator’s events seems to be the audience’s favourite element? Answer: Based on the text, the audience’s favourite part is when the fake police arrive, collect evidence, and question the main suspects. Explicación de la paráfrasis: La opción habla de “the arrival of the ‘police,’ who gather evidence and interrogate suspects” . La respuesta correcta lo expresa de forma más natural y más simple: “when the fake police arrive, collect evidence, and question the main suspects” . Aquí cambias “interrogate” por “question” , que es más ## B1, y “gather evidence” por “collect evidence” , que mantiene el mismo significado. La idea clave es: la parte favorita del público es la llegada de la policía y la investigación . ## IDIOMS # 1. Original: The politician avoided answering the question directly. Rephrase using an idiom related to crime: The politician ______
